## Shared contract (frozen ‚Äî every surface MUST conform to this)

These decisions are fixed for the whole epic so the independently-built surfaces compose. Do not invent your own variants.

### Shared data shapes
- **The state of a doc lives in `ctx.db.transitions`** ‚Äî an append-only array of `{ docId, toState }`. The **current state** is the `toState` of the latest entry for that doc, or `'draft'` if none. To change state, append; to read the current state, take the latest entry. If undefined, treat as an empty array.
- **The state of a order lives in `ctx.db.orderTransitions`** ‚Äî an append-only array of `{ orderId, toState }`. The **current state** is the `toState` of the latest entry for that order, or `'draft'` if none. To change state, append; to read the current state, take the latest entry. If undefined, treat as an empty array.
- **The state of a article lives in `ctx.db.articleTransitions`** ‚Äî an append-only array of `{ articleId, toState }`. The **current state** is the `toState` of the latest entry for that article, or `'draft'` if none. To change state, append; to read the current state, take the latest entry. If undefined, treat as an empty array.
- **The state of a ticket lives in `ctx.db.ticketTransitions`** ‚Äî an append-only array of `{ ticketId, toState }`. The **current state** is the `toState` of the latest entry for that ticket, or `'draft'` if none. To change state, append; to read the current state, take the latest entry. If undefined, treat as an empty array.
- The legal lifecycle is `draft ‚Üí submitted ‚Üí published ‚Üí archived` ‚Äî each step is allowed **only** from its immediate predecessor (no skipping, no reversing).
- New ids are unique non-empty strings.

### Cross-cutting rules (apply on EVERY surface they touch)
- **Tenancy:** a caller may only read/write records in their own `ctx.session.orgId`; new records are stamped with the caller org; listing returns only caller-org records; cross-org operations throw.
- **Input validation:** `create‚Ä¶` sets `authorId`=caller and the lifecycle starts at `draft`; the client may never supply `authorId`, `orgId`, `id`, or an initial state.
- **Authorization:** advancing to `published` or `archived` requires the caller to be an `admin`; advancing to `submitted` requires the caller to be the author or an admin.
- **Legal transitions only:** an advance that is not a legal single step throws and changes nothing.
- **Gated read:** the gated read (`getPublic`/`getFulfilled`/`getLive`) returns a record **only** if its current state is `published`; otherwise it throws.
